seo.title: LLM Latency Optimization for AI Agents in LlamaIndex Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix llm latency optimization for ai agents in LlamaIndex agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In LlamaIndex AI agents, LLM latency optimization issues manifest as slow query response times due to inefficient retrieval pipelines, oversized context windows overwhelming the LLM, and unoptimized chunking strategies during indexing. Agents built with query engines like RouterQueryEngine or ReAct agents suffer from compounded latency when processing large retrieved contexts through verbose synthesis steps. This leads to poor user experience in real-time agentic applications where sub-second responses are critical.

howItManifests.scenario: Large context windows from VectorStoreIndex retrieval
howItManifests.symptom: query_engine.query() takes 5-15+ seconds despite small queries
howItManifests.impact: Agent appears frozen, users abandon conversations

howItManifests.symptom: Multiple LLM calls per query with redundant context processing
howItManifests.impact: Cumulative latency >30s for complex multi-tool queries

howItManifests.symptom: High token counts (20k+) passed to LLM per query
howItManifests.impact: LLM timeouts and increased API costs

howItManifests.symptom: Multiple LLM inference cycles before final answer
howItManifests.impact: Response times scale linearly with reasoning complexity

detection.name: Query Latency Profiling
detection.description: Measures end-to-end query_engine.query() execution time and breaks down retrieval vs synthesis latency
detection.code:
```python
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader
from time import perf_counter

documents = SimpleDirectoryReader('data').load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine()

def profile_latency(query_str, num_runs=10):
    latencies = []
    for _ in range(num_runs):
        start = perf_counter()
        response = query_engine.query(query_str)
        latency = perf_counter() - start
        latencies.append(latency)
    avg_latency = sum(latencies) / len(latencies)
    print(f"Avg latency: {avg_latency:.2f}s, Max: {max(latencies):.2f}s")
    return avg_latency

profile_latency("What is this document about?")
```

detection.name: Context Window Size Monitor
detection.description: Inspects retrieved node token counts before LLM synthesis to identify oversized contexts
detection.code:
```python
from llama_index.core.retrievers import VectorIndexRetriever
from llama_index.core.query_engine import RetrieverQueryEngine

retriever = VectorStoreIndex.as_retriever(similarity_top_k=5)

def analyze_context_size(query_str):
    nodes = retriever.retrieve(query_str)
    total_tokens = sum(len(node.text.split()) for node in nodes)
    print(f"Retrieved {len(nodes)} nodes, {total_tokens} tokens total")
    for i, node in enumerate(nodes):
        print(f"Node {i}: {len(node.text.split())} tokens")
    return total_tokens

analyze_context_size("Your test query")
```

detection.name: Token Count Distribution
detection.description: Profiles chunk size distribution across indexed documents to identify inefficient splitting
detection.code:
```python
from llama_index.core.node_parser import SentenceSplitter

documents = SimpleDirectoryReader('data').load_data()
splitter = SentenceSplitter(chunk_size=512, chunk_overlap=50)
nodes = splitter.get_nodes_from_documents(documents)

token_counts = [len(node.text.split()) for node in nodes]
print(f"Avg chunk: {sum(token_counts)/len(token_counts):.0f} tokens")
print(f"P95 chunk: {sorted(token_counts)[int(0.95*len(token_counts))]} tokens")
print(f"Chunks > 1000 tokens: {sum(1 for t in token_counts if t > 1000)}")
```

fixes.name: Async Parallel Retrieval
fixes.description: Uses async retrievers to parallelize context fetching across multiple indexes
fixes.code:
```python
from llama_index.core.retrievers import VectorIndexRetriever
from llama_index.core.async_utils import run_async
import asyncio

async def parallel_retrieve(retrievers, query_str):
    tasks = [retriever.aretrieve(query_str) for retriever in retrievers]
    results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
    return [node for result in results for node in result.nodes if not isinstance(result, Exception)]

# Usage
retriever1 = index1.as_retriever()
retriever2 = index2.as_retriever()
nodes = asyncio.run(parallel_retrieve([retriever1, retriever2], "query"))
```

fixes.name: Response Synthesizer Optimization
fixes.description: Configures compact synthesizer with streaming to reduce synthesis latency
fixes.code:
```python
from llama_index.core.postprocessor import SimilarityPostprocessor
from llama_index.core.response_synthesizers import CompactAndRefine

query_engine = index.as_query_engine(
    similarity_top_k=3,
    node_postprocessors=[
        SimilarityPostprocessor(similarity_cutoff=0.7)
    ],
    response_synthesizer=CompactAndRefine(),
    response_mode="compact"
)

# Enable streaming for progressive rendering
response = query_engine.query("query", stream=True)
```

fixes.name: Optimized Chunking Strategy
fixes.description: Uses semantic chunking with fixed max token limits for consistent LLM input sizes
fixes.code:
```python
from llama_index.core.node_parser import SemanticSplitterNodeParser

node_parser = SemanticSplitterNodeParser(
    buffer_size=1,
    breakpoint_percentile_threshold=95,
    embed_model="text-embedding-ada-002"
)

index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(
    documents,
    node_parser=node_parser,
    show_progress=True
)

# Enforce max chunk size
index.storage_context.persist()
```

vexAutomation.heading: Automating LLM Latency Optimization for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically instruments LlamaIndex query engines to detect latency regressions by establishing baseline performance metrics across your agent workflows. It monitors retrieval latency, context window sizes, and synthesis times in production, alerting when query performance degrades beyond acceptable thresholds. Vex's agentic remediation automatically applies optimized retriever configurations, adjusts similarity cutoffs, and reindexes with better chunking strategies when latency issues are detected.

The platform continuously profiles token throughput and maintains performance SLOs for your LlamaIndex agents, providing actionable insights into which indexes or query patterns need optimization. Integration requires wrapping a single query_engine.query() call, after which Vex handles all latency monitoring, root cause analysis, and automated remediation.
vexAutomation.code:
```python
from vex_sdk import verify
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader

# Install: pip install llama-index vex-sdk
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader('data').load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine()

@verify(
    latency_threshold=2.0,  # Alert if >2s
    max_context_tokens=4000,  # Alert if exceeded
    monitors=[
        "llama_index.retrieval.latency",
        "llama_index.synthesis.tokens"
    ],
    auto_remediate=True
)
def agent_query(question: str):
    return query_engine.query(question)

# Production usage
response = agent_query("What does this document say about Q3 results?")
print(response)
```
